text.title: Semantic XML Content — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every content/*.xml file must obey. Source: Anthropic prompt guidance on XML, internal faion-network conventions.

rule.statement:
Tag names describe role, not appearance. ``, ``, `` are allowed; ``, ``, `
` are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Agents act on roles; formatting tags carry no semantic information and waste tokens.

rule.statement: All tags must appear in `templates/tag-glossary.xml`. New tags require a glossary PR that documents purpose, attributes, and parents/children.
rule.rationale: Open vocabularies diverge across authors; closed glossary keeps the corpus diff-able and parseable by one schema.

rule.statement: Source code lives inside `` with a CDATA wrapper. Inline `<` `>` `&` in element text are escaped with `<` `>` `&`.
rule.rationale: Unescaped characters break parsers; CDATA lets code blocks contain any character without escaping.

rule.statement: Root element is ``. First child is `` (one sentence). No file ships without these.
rule.rationale: Indexers and validators key off the root + summary; absence breaks tooling.

rule.statement: One concept per file. A file approaching 150 lines is split into siblings (`05a-foo.xml`, `05b-bar.xml`).
rule.rationale: 200+ line files exceed the agent's load budget and stop being routing-friendly.
